_comment: pyrrtm-lite configuration file. Lines starting with _comment are ignored by the reader.
_comment_usage: Copy this file, fill in your values, then run: python pyrrtm_lite.py --config myconfig.json --input sounding.nc --output fluxes.nc

surface._comment: Surface boundary conditions applied to every profile
surface._comment_sw_albedo: Broadband SW surface albedo [0-1, dimensionless]. Nominal values: ocean 0.06, grass/cropland 0.15-0.25, forest 0.10-0.15, desert/bare soil 0.25-0.40, fresh snow 0.80-0.90
surface._comment_skin_temp: Surface skin temperature [°C]. Set null to use the lowest-level air temperature from the sounding (reasonable for land; may be off over ocean)

gases._comment: Greenhouse gas volume mixing ratios applied uniformly to all levels. USSA76 profiles are used for O3; N2O and CO are set to USSA76 defaults.
gases._comment_CH4: CH4 [ppm]. Current atmosphere ~1.9; pre-industrial 0.72; high-emission scenario ~3.5

solar._comment: Solar geometry. Latitude/longitude are used to compute solar zenith angle from the sounding time stamps. If fixed_sza_deg is set it overrides the computed angle (useful for single-profile sensitivity tests).
solar._comment_latitude: Site latitude [degrees N]. Set null to read from the sounding NetCDF (variable 'lat')
solar._comment_longitude: Site longitude [degrees E, -180 to 180]. Set null to read from the sounding NetCDF (variable 'lon')
solar._comment_fixed_sza: If not null, use this solar zenith angle [degrees] for every profile instead of computing it from time+location. Range 0 (overhead) to 90 (horizon). Profiles where SZA>=90 are set to zero SW flux regardless.

vertical_grid._comment: Output flux profiles are reported on a uniform grid below 15 km and a 1-km grid from 15 to 60 km. The sounding is interpolated onto this grid before running the model.
vertical_grid._comment_resolution: Vertical resolution of output flux profiles below 15 km [km]. Nominal values: 0.25 (fine, ~120 levels total), 0.5 (standard, ~76 levels), 1.0 (coarse, ~60 levels). Finer resolution increases run time linearly.

output._comment_clearsky: true = write both all-sky and clear-sky flux profiles (lw_dn, lw_up, sw_dn, sw_up AND lw_dn_clr, lw_up_clr, sw_dn_clr, sw_up_clr). false = all-sky only. Clear-sky run doubles compute time.

processing._comment_cpu: Number of CPU cores to use. -1 = all logical cores on the machine. Recommended: 4-8 for a workstation, 16-32 for a cluster node. Set 1 to disable parallelism (useful for debugging).
